_comment_2: Canonical p09 contract: gateway injects $.bench.injected=true and drops $.secret on the JSON request body before forwarding to upstream.
_comment_3: Implemented via Tyk's NATIVE `transform` middleware (mw_transform.go) — Go text/template + the bundled Sprig v3 FuncMap. Sprig provides `unset` / `set` / `dict` / `mustToJson` / `hasKey` / `index`, which between them give us JSON-aware dotted-path mutation without leaving the Go runtime: no otto JSVM, no MiniRequestObject (un)marshal, no per-request VM context switch. Sprig is wired in apidef/api_definitions.go via APIDefinitionLoader.filterSprigFuncs (the only stripped funcs are the env-leak pair env/expandenv).
_comment_4: Earlier iterations of this profile used a JSVM `pre` middleware (body_rewrite_request.js) reading config_data. That worked but added enough per-request overhead that p11's 1200 rps burst probe could not saturate global_rate_limit (otto caps Tyk at ~830 rps on this hardware, well below the 1000 rps RL threshold). The migration to native `transform` removes that overhead end-to-end and lets the same template back both p09 and p11. The shared template lives at _shared/templates/p10_request_rewrite.tmpl.
_comment_5: Method=POST only: mw_transform.go reads/parses the body unconditionally on a path/method match. The fixture only sends POSTs; registering on GET/HEAD/OPTIONS/DELETE would force a body-parse round-trip on bodyless methods for no semantic gain. Same defensive method-guard pattern p11 uses.
name: bench
